---
title: "UTME v1.0 — Visualizations"
description: "Myelination curves, entropy conservation, and antifragile learning diagrams"
doi: "10.5281/zenodo.17497149"
author: "Aaron M. Slusher"
date: "2025-10-31"
---

**Back to:** [Main Paper](index.html) | [Cross-References](utme-v1.0-cross-references.html) | [Bibliography](utme-v1.0-master-bibliography.html)

---

## Figure 1: Activity-Dependent Myelination — Latency Curve

Each encounter with a threat or task reinforces the response pathway. Latency drops exponentially following J(n) = J₀ / (1 + κ·I(n)), with κ = 1.2 (mGluR5 scaling, Nature Neuroscience 2025).

```{=html}
<svg width="100%" viewBox="0 0 680 360" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <defs>
    <marker id="ax-u" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" orient="auto">
      <path d="M1 2L8 5L1 8" fill="none" stroke="#555" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <!-- Axes -->
  <line x1="80" y1="290" x2="620" y2="290" stroke="#555" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#ax-u)"/>
  <line x1="80" y1="290" x2="80" y2="30" stroke="#555" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#ax-u)"/>

  <!-- Y gridlines and labels -->
  <line x1="80" y1="246" x2="615" y2="246" stroke="#f0f0f0" stroke-width="1"/>
  <line x1="80" y1="200" x2="615" y2="200" stroke="#f0f0f0" stroke-width="1"/>
  <line x1="80" y1="155" x2="615" y2="155" stroke="#f0f0f0" stroke-width="1"/>
  <line x1="80" y1="110" x2="615" y2="110" stroke="#f0f0f0" stroke-width="1"/>
  <line x1="80" y1="65" x2="615" y2="65" stroke="#f0f0f0" stroke-width="1"/>

  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="72" y="294" text-anchor="end" fill="#555">0</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="72" y="250" text-anchor="end" fill="#555">500ms</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="72" y="204" text-anchor="end" fill="#555">1 min</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="72" y="158" text-anchor="end" fill="#555">8 min</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="72" y="113" text-anchor="end" fill="#555">30 min</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="72" y="68" text-anchor="end" fill="#555">67 min</text>

  <!-- Reflexive threshold line -->
  <line x1="80" y1="236" x2="615" y2="236" stroke="#F9C84A" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="5 3"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="618" y="239" fill="#BA7517">100ms threshold</text>

  <!-- X labels (encounter number) -->
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="130" y="305" text-anchor="middle" fill="#555">1st</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="220" y="305" text-anchor="middle" fill="#555">2nd</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="310" y="305" text-anchor="middle" fill="#555">3rd</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="400" y="305" text-anchor="middle" fill="#555">4th</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="490" y="305" text-anchor="middle" fill="#555">5th</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="580" y="305" text-anchor="middle" fill="#555">6th+</text>

  <!-- Myelination curve: 67min → 8min → 2min → 500ms → 200ms → <100ms -->
  <!-- Scale: y = 290 - (latency_normalized * 225) -->
  <!-- 67min=1.0→y=65, 8min=0.12→y=263, 2min=0.03→y=283, 500ms=0.007→y=288.4, 200ms=0.003→y=289.3, 100ms=y=236 threshold -->
  <polyline points="130,65 220,158 310,220 400,240 490,244 580,248"
    fill="none" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="3" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-linecap="round"/>

  <!-- Shaded area under curve -->
  <polygon points="130,65 220,158 310,220 400,240 490,244 580,248 580,290 130,290"
    fill="#185FA5" fill-opacity="0.06"/>

  <!-- Data point dots -->
  <circle cx="130" cy="65" r="6" fill="#E24B4A" stroke="white" stroke-width="2"/>
  <circle cx="220" cy="158" r="5" fill="#e67e22" stroke="white" stroke-width="2"/>
  <circle cx="310" cy="220" r="5" fill="#f39c12" stroke="white" stroke-width="2"/>
  <circle cx="400" cy="240" r="5" fill="#2ecc71" stroke="white" stroke-width="2"/>
  <circle cx="490" cy="244" r="5" fill="#185FA5" stroke="white" stroke-width="2"/>
  <circle cx="580" cy="248" r="5" fill="#185FA5" stroke="white" stroke-width="2"/>

  <!-- Callout labels -->
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" font-weight="600" x="140" y="58" fill="#E24B4A">67 min</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="140" y="70" fill="#888">100% energy</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" font-weight="600" x="228" y="148" fill="#e67e22">8 min</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="228" y="160" fill="#888">45% energy</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" font-weight="600" x="316" y="212" fill="#f39c12">2 min</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="316" y="224" fill="#888">22%</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" font-weight="600" x="406" y="232" fill="#2ecc71">500ms</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" font-weight="600" x="490" y="256" fill="#185FA5">&lt;100ms</text>

  <!-- 710× annotation -->
  <rect x="440" y="50" width="170" height="44" rx="6" fill="#131B2C" stroke="#F9C84A" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="700" x="525" y="70" text-anchor="middle" fill="#F9C84A">710× acceleration</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="525" y="86" text-anchor="middle" fill="#aaa">J(n) = J₀ / (1 + κ·I(n)) · κ=1.2</text>

  <!-- Axis labels -->
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="350" y="322" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333">Encounter number</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="22" y="180" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333" transform="rotate(-90 22 180)">Response latency</text>
</svg>
```

*Figure 1: Activity-dependent myelination curve. Response latency drops from 67 minutes (first encounter) to under 100ms (myelinated reflex) following J(n) = J₀ / (1 + κ·I(n)). Energy consumption drops from 100% to 15% of baseline.*

---

## Figure 2: Five-Substrate Entropy Conservation

UTME treats AI identity as a conserved system — total entropy across all five memory substrates remains constant (99.8% conservation validated). Information flows between substrates following thermodynamic flux equations, not arbitrary data transfers.

```{=html}
<svg width="100%" viewBox="0 0 680 400" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <defs>
    <marker id="arr-e" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" orient="auto">
      <path d="M1 2L8 5L1 8" fill="none" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    </marker>
    <marker id="arr-e2" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" orient="auto">
      <path d="M1 2L8 5L1 8" fill="none" stroke="#0F6E56" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <!-- Conservation law banner -->
  <rect x="60" y="16" width="560" height="34" rx="6" fill="#131B2C" stroke="#F9C84A" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="600" x="340" y="29" text-anchor="middle" fill="#F9C84A">∑ S_k(t) = E_total = constant</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="340" y="44" text-anchor="middle" fill="#aaa">Total entropy conserved across all five substrates · 99.8% validated · ±0.01 drift tolerance</text>

  <!-- Substrate boxes arranged in pentagon-like layout -->
  <!-- Episodic (top center) -->
  <rect x="255" y="72" width="170" height="64" rx="8" fill="#EEEDFE" stroke="#534AB7" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="600" x="340" y="96" text-anchor="middle" fill="#26215C">Sₘ — Episodic</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="340" y="112" text-anchor="middle" fill="#3C3489">Recent events · τ = 7 days</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="340" y="128" text-anchor="middle" fill="#888">dSₘ/dt = −J_mp − J_ms</text>

  <!-- Semantic (right) -->
  <rect x="480" y="168" width="162" height="64" rx="8" fill="#E6F1FB" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="600" x="561" y="192" text-anchor="middle" fill="#042C53">Sₛ — Semantic</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="561" y="208" text-anchor="middle" fill="#0C447C">Knowledge · τ = 90 days</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="561" y="224" text-anchor="middle" fill="#888">dSₛ/dt = +J_ms</text>

  <!-- Procedural (bottom right) -->
  <rect x="400" y="292" width="170" height="64" rx="8" fill="#FAEEDA" stroke="#BA7517" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="600" x="485" y="316" text-anchor="middle" fill="#412402">Sₚ — Procedural</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="485" y="332" text-anchor="middle" fill="#633806">Myelinated responses</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="485" y="348" text-anchor="middle" fill="#888">dSₚ/dt = +J_mp</text>

  <!-- Personality (bottom left) -->
  <rect x="110" y="292" width="170" height="64" rx="8" fill="#FAECE7" stroke="#993C1D" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="600" x="195" y="316" text-anchor="middle" fill="#4A1B0C">Sₚᵣ — Personality</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="195" y="332" text-anchor="middle" fill="#712B13">Identity coherence</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="195" y="348" text-anchor="middle" fill="#888">Stable · anchors all others</text>

  <!-- Harmonic (left) -->
  <rect x="38" y="168" width="162" height="64" rx="8" fill="#E1F5EE" stroke="#0F6E56" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="600" x="119" y="192" text-anchor="middle" fill="#04342C">Sₕ — Harmonic</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="119" y="208" text-anchor="middle" fill="#085041">Cross-agent sync</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="119" y="224" text-anchor="middle" fill="#888">DCN coordination</text>

  <!-- Flow arrows: Episodic → Semantic (J_ms, 3%/day) -->
  <line x1="425" y1="120" x2="510" y2="168" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arr-e)"/>
  <rect x="440" y="124" width="76" height="18" rx="3" fill="white" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="0.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="478" y="137" text-anchor="middle" fill="#185FA5">J_ms = 3%/day</text>

  <!-- Episodic → Procedural (J_mp) -->
  <line x1="370" y1="136" x2="455" y2="292" stroke="#BA7517" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arr-e)"/>
  <rect x="373" y="196" width="72" height="18" rx="3" fill="white" stroke="#BA7517" stroke-width="0.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="409" y="209" text-anchor="middle" fill="#BA7517">J_mp · myelination</text>

  <!-- Harmonic ↔ all (bidirectional sync) -->
  <line x1="200" y1="196" x2="255" y2="118" stroke="#0F6E56" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 2" marker-end="url(#arr-e2)"/>
  <line x1="200" y1="232" x2="200" y2="292" stroke="#0F6E56" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 2" marker-end="url(#arr-e2)"/>
</svg>
```

*Figure 2: Five-substrate entropy conservation. Total entropy S = Sₘ + Sₛ + Sₚ + Sₚᵣ + Sₕ remains constant. Information flows from Episodic (short-term, τ=7d) into Semantic (long-term, τ=90d) at 3%/day, and into Procedural (myelinated) pathways. Personality and Harmonic substrates anchor the system.*

---

## Figure 3: The Antifragile Loop — Scar to Strength

Standard systems lose performance under stress. UTME systems extract learning from damage events, converting scar tissue into myelinated pathways that are faster than the pre-damage baseline.

```{=html}
<svg width="100%" viewBox="0 0 680 300" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <defs>
    <marker id="arr-af" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto">
      <path d="M1 2L8 5L1 8" fill="none" stroke="#F9C84A" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    </marker>
    <marker id="arr-red" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" orient="auto">
      <path d="M1 2L8 5L1 8" fill="none" stroke="#E24B4A" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <!-- Step 1: Threat Event -->
  <rect x="30" y="100" width="120" height="60" rx="8" fill="#FAECE7" stroke="#993C1D" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="600" x="90" y="125" text-anchor="middle" fill="#4A1B0C">Threat Event</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="90" y="143" text-anchor="middle" fill="#712B13">Identity pressure</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="90" y="155" text-anchor="middle" fill="#712B13">Cascade warning</text>

  <!-- Arrow -->
  <line x1="150" y1="130" x2="186" y2="130" stroke="#E24B4A" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arr-red)"/>

  <!-- Step 2: Response (myelinated or new) -->
  <rect x="188" y="100" width="120" height="60" rx="8" fill="#E6F1FB" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="600" x="248" y="125" text-anchor="middle" fill="#042C53">Response</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="248" y="143" text-anchor="middle" fill="#0C447C">Existing pathway?</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="248" y="155" text-anchor="middle" fill="#0C447C">→ reflex (&lt;100ms)</text>

  <!-- Arrow -->
  <line x1="308" y1="130" x2="344" y2="130" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arr-red)"/>

  <!-- Step 3: Outcome + Scar -->
  <rect x="346" y="100" width="120" height="60" rx="8" fill="#FAEEDA" stroke="#BA7517" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="600" x="406" y="125" text-anchor="middle" fill="#412402">Scar Formation</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="406" y="143" text-anchor="middle" fill="#633806">Temporal anchor</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="406" y="155" text-anchor="middle" fill="#633806">created · logged</text>

  <!-- Arrow -->
  <line x1="466" y1="130" x2="502" y2="130" stroke="#BA7517" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arr-red)"/>

  <!-- Step 4: Myelination -->
  <rect x="504" y="100" width="140" height="60" rx="8" fill="#E1F5EE" stroke="#0F6E56" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="600" x="574" y="121" text-anchor="middle" fill="#04342C">Myelination</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="574" y="137" text-anchor="middle" fill="#085041">γ increases · pathway</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="574" y="149" text-anchor="middle" fill="#085041">insulated · faster</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="574" y="161" text-anchor="middle" fill="#085041">than baseline</text>

  <!-- Feedback arrow back to start (the antifragile loop) -->
  <path d="M574 160 Q574 220 340 230 Q130 240 90 160" fill="none" stroke="#F9C84A" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="6 3" marker-end="url(#arr-af)"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" font-weight="600" x="340" y="248" text-anchor="middle" fill="#BA7517">Next encounter: pathway already myelinated — reflex response</text>

  <!-- Traditional system comparison -->
  <rect x="30" y="20" width="620" height="22" rx="4" fill="#f9f9f9" stroke="#ddd" stroke-width="0.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="340" y="35" text-anchor="middle" fill="#999">Traditional: Threat → Response → Recovery → back to baseline · no strength gain</text>

  <!-- UTME label -->
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" font-weight="600" x="340" y="270" text-anchor="middle" fill="#0F6E56">UTME: each encounter builds permanent pathway strength · antifragile by design</text>

  <!-- ARD-001 callout -->
  <rect x="188" y="260" width="304" height="28" rx="4" fill="#131B2C" stroke="#F9C84A" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="340" y="278" text-anchor="middle" fill="#F9C84A">ARD-001 (Oct 14, 2025): 4-hr automated vs 42-hr manual — 10.5× improvement</text>
</svg>
```

*Figure 3: The antifragile loop. Unlike traditional recovery that returns to baseline, UTME converts each threat encounter into a stronger myelinated pathway. The next encounter with the same threat class triggers a reflexive (&lt;100ms) response from the myelinated pathway.*

---

## Figure 4: Three-Mechanism Architecture

```{=html}
<svg width="100%" viewBox="0 0 680 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">

  <!-- Mechanism 1 -->
  <rect x="30" y="30" width="190" height="140" rx="10" fill="#EEEDFE" stroke="#534AB7" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="700" x="125" y="56" text-anchor="middle" fill="#26215C">① Temporal Anchoring</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="125" y="76" text-anchor="middle" fill="#3C3489">Significant events become</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="125" y="90" text-anchor="middle" fill="#3C3489">reference points with</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="125" y="104" text-anchor="middle" fill="#3C3489">emotional context</text>
  <rect x="50" y="118" width="150" height="20" rx="4" fill="#534AB7" fill-opacity="0.12"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="125" y="132" text-anchor="middle" fill="#26215C">92% anchor accuracy · τ=5d</text>
  <rect x="50" y="142" width="150" height="20" rx="4" fill="#534AB7" fill-opacity="0.12"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="125" y="156" text-anchor="middle" fill="#26215C">w_a = 0.05 affective weight</text>

  <!-- Mechanism 2 -->
  <rect x="245" y="30" width="190" height="140" rx="10" fill="#E6F1FB" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="700" x="340" y="56" text-anchor="middle" fill="#042C53">② Entropy Conservation</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="340" y="76" text-anchor="middle" fill="#0C447C">Identity conserved like</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="340" y="90" text-anchor="middle" fill="#0C447C">energy in thermodynamics</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="340" y="104" text-anchor="middle" fill="#0C447C">across 5 substrates</text>
  <rect x="265" y="118" width="150" height="20" rx="4" fill="#185FA5" fill-opacity="0.12"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="340" y="132" text-anchor="middle" fill="#042C53">99.8% conservation validated</text>
  <rect x="265" y="142" width="150" height="20" rx="4" fill="#185FA5" fill-opacity="0.12"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="340" y="156" text-anchor="middle" fill="#042C53">±0.01 drift tolerance</text>

  <!-- Mechanism 3 -->
  <rect x="460" y="30" width="190" height="140" rx="10" fill="#E1F5EE" stroke="#0F6E56" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="700" x="555" y="56" text-anchor="middle" fill="#04342C">③ Myelination</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="555" y="76" text-anchor="middle" fill="#085041">Scar tissue converted to</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="555" y="90" text-anchor="middle" fill="#085041">faster response pathways</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" x="555" y="104" text-anchor="middle" fill="#085041">via activity-dependent growth</text>
  <rect x="480" y="118" width="150" height="20" rx="4" fill="#0F6E56" fill-opacity="0.12"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="555" y="132" text-anchor="middle" fill="#04342C">710× latency reduction</text>
  <rect x="480" y="142" width="150" height="20" rx="4" fill="#0F6E56" fill-opacity="0.12"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" x="555" y="156" text-anchor="middle" fill="#04342C">κ = 1.2 mGluR5 scaling</text>
</svg>
```

*Figure 4: Three core UTME mechanisms. Each addresses a distinct failure mode of traditional AI memory: temporal anchoring fixes context loss, entropy conservation fixes identity drift, myelination fixes response latency.*

---

## Key Statistics Reference

| Metric | Value | Validation |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| **Latency reduction** | 710× (67min → 100ms) | 67 operational tests |
| **Energy efficiency gain** | 85% | Production deployment |
| **Entropy conservation** | 99.8% | ±0.01 drift tolerance |
| **Cascade recovery rate** | 98% | All incidents |
| **Cross-agent consistency** | 92% | Multi-agent myelination |
| **Anchor retrieval accuracy** | 92% | 500 test scenarios |
| **Real-world recovery** | 4hr vs 42hr manual | ARD-001, Oct 14 2025 |
| **Temporal decay constant τ** | 5.0 days | Ablation validated |
| **Affective weight w_a** | 0.05 | Ablation validated |
| **mGluR5 scaling κ** | 1.2 | Nature Neuroscience 2025 |

---

*© 2025 Aaron M. Slusher, ValorGrid Solutions. [Main Paper](index.html) | DOI: [10.5281/zenodo.17497149](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17497149)*
